meta.title: EU Resettlement Programmes 2026
meta.description: Official EU and member state resettlement programmes that bring vulnerable refugees safely to Europe — through UNHCR referral.

hero.title: EU Resettlement Programmes
hero.sub: The European Union and its member states run official resettlement programmes that bring the world's most vulnerable refugees directly to Europe — safely, legally, on a plane. You do not need to pay a smuggler. You do not need to cross the Mediterranean. Register with UNHCR where you are.

alert.text: EU resettlement is always completely free. If anyone asks you to pay to be considered for a resettlement programme — they are scamming you. Report them to UNHCR immediately.

steps.title: Register with UNHCR
steps.desc: Register with UNHCR at the nearest office in your country of refuge. This is free and essential. Bring any documents you have — but lack of documents does not disqualify you.

steps.title: UNHCR assessment
steps.desc: UNHCR assesses your situation and identifies whether you have protection needs, vulnerabilities, and resettlement prospects. This process can take 1–3 years.

steps.title: UNHCR referral to EU country
steps.desc: UNHCR refers your case to an EU member state that has pledged resettlement places. You cannot choose which country — UNHCR and the member state decide based on fit and available places.

steps.title: EU country processing
steps.desc: The EU country conducts its own security and health checks, interviews, and approvals. This takes several months to over a year after referral.

steps.title: Travel to Europe
steps.desc: Once approved, travel is arranged and paid for by IOM or the receiving country. You arrive with refugee status, housing provided, and an integration support package.

note: UNHCR refers only the most vulnerable. Resettlement is not available to everyone — it is a lifeline for those facing the most serious protection threats. If you are in a safe country with basic stability, focus on legal economic migration or education routes instead.
